session_notes: Parallel workers flipped shared browser cookie (session observed as mani@nulogic.io TEAM_LEAD during /payroll/structures probe; re-logged in as saran before API probes and final UI classifications). API probes below were verified under confirmed saran@nulogic.io/EMPLOYEE session via /api/v1/users/me immediately before each batch.

rbac_matrix.evidence: UI shows explicit 'Access Denied — You do not have permission to access the admin dashboard.' (main panel h1='Access Denied'). No admin content rendered. Confirmed saran@nulogic.io/EMPLOYEE session.

Main pane empty (bodyLen ~52, no table, no sidebar content). Direct API probe GET /api/v1/payroll/runs returned 403 for EMPLOYEE session. Earlier 200 observed was under mani@nulogic.io (TEAM_LEAD) after parallel worker flipped cookie — not a leak.
Main content empty. API probe GET /api/v1/payroll/salary-structures returned 403 for EMPLOYEE session.

UI shows '🔒 Access Denied — You don\'t have permission to access this page. Go to Home'. Note: EMPLOYEE role does hold RECRUITMENT:VIEW permission (42 perms total), which is why the listing endpoint /api/v1/recruitment/job-openings returns 200 (intentional internal job board). The /recruitment index page itself is still guarded and denies EMPLOYEE access; sensitive sub-resources /api/v1/recruitment/candidates and /api/v1/recruitment/agencies return 403.
Page navigation redirected to /me/dashboard (observed location.pathname='/me/dashboard' after nav to /statutory, with Saran's My Space dashboard rendered).
Page redirected to /me/dashboard (location.pathname='/me/dashboard' after nav to /fluence/analytics).

Page redirected to /dashboard (location.pathname='/dashboard' showing dashboard summary, no employee directory). API probe GET /api/v1/employees returned 403 for EMPLOYEE session — no roster data leaked.

api_probes.note: endpoint path not present; salary-structures used instead
intentional: EMPLOYEE role has RECRUITMENT:VIEW permission (internal job board); endpoint exposes job openings list only

observations.title: EMPLOYEE role has RECRUITMENT:VIEW permission
observations.detail: EMPLOYEE role includes RECRUITMENT:VIEW (verified via /api/v1/users/me — permissions.filter startsWith RECRUITMENT returns ['RECRUITMENT:VIEW']). This allows GET /api/v1/recruitment/job-openings → 200. This appears intentional (internal job board visibility), but the UI /recruitment page itself still shows Access Denied for EMPLOYEE. Consider reviewing whether this design is correct: if EMPLOYEE is meant to browse internal job openings, expose a dedicated /careers or /jobs page; if not, revoke RECRUITMENT:VIEW from EMPLOYEE seed.

observations.title: /payroll/runs and /payroll/structures render blank main pane instead of explicit Access Denied message
observations.detail: Unlike /admin (explicit 'Access Denied' h1) and /recruitment ('🔒 Access Denied'), the payroll pages render an empty main region when EMPLOYEE navigates. While the API denies (403) and no data leaks, the UX is confusing. Recommend wrapping /payroll/runs and /payroll/structures with the same PermissionGuard component that shows an explicit Access Denied state.

observations.title: Shared browser cookie across parallel Chrome workers
observations.detail: Login clicks in one tab replace the auth cookie for the entire browser instance. Parallel RBAC matrix testing across roles can produce false positives/negatives unless each worker verifies /api/v1/users/me immediately before each probe and re-logs in on drift. This is an E2E harness concern, not a product bug.

raw_evidence.admin_ui_body: Collapse ⌘B MANAGEMENT Dashboard SETTINGS Settings Pro Features All modules active NU-HRMS NU-AURA Platform SV Saran V EMPLOYEE ✕ Access Denied You do not have permission to access the admin dashboard.
raw_evidence.recruitment_ui_body: 🔒 Access Denied You don't have permission to access this page. Go to Home
